_source: Narrative system design §4. Sprint 5 expansion: 6 quotes per role-mood cell = 108 total (was 18 in Phase 1 scaffold). AdvisorSystem.generate_quote() picks one by hash-seeded pseudo-random selection per event.
_note: Species personality flavor is applied as an optional substitution by AdvisorSystem.generate_quote() based on the advisor's species. The base quote is the fallback.
_style: First-person advisor voice, addressing the captain. Terse, characterful. NO em dashes in new lines (project style rule). Single quotes for nested speech.

positive: I know someone who deals in these. Could be worth triple on the right world.
Margins on this are obscene. We move fast, we eat well for a month.
Black market wants these badly. Don't ask why.
Found a buyer before we even hit ground. The price comes pre-negotiated.
Patron's contact owes me a favor. I'm calling it in.
Tag it, crate it, sell it. Easy credits, no questions.
warning: Those markings are Unity military. Could be trouble.
Whoever stamped this didn't want it traced. We hold or we burn it.
That broker is going to undercut us if we don't pivot now.
Stamps don't match. Either it's stolen or it's a frame.
Spotted a Crimson Collective tag on the manifest. They'll come asking.
Local buyers all dropped quotes within an hour. Cartel pressure.
neutral: Standard merchant fare. Nothing special.
Spot price is what it is. Could go either way next cycle.
Off-world tariffs are dragging the margin. We break even at best.
Two buyers, one price. Take it or wait for the next port.
Catalogue value is honest. No upside, no downside.
Nothing flagged. Nothing flagged usually means nothing flagged.

positive: Patch jobs hold. They'll be back on their feet by morning.
Clean break. Set, splinted, healing well.
Vitals are textbook. Don't get used to it.
That dose worked. Pulse is strong, color is coming back.
Sutures held the trip. Good tissue response.
They walked out under their own power. That's the win.
warning: That wound is going to fester if we don't get supplies soon.
Pulse is dropping. We need a medbay, not a field kit.
Internal bleeding. I'm buying time, not fixing this.
Antibiotics are out. The next infection kills someone.
Concussion symptoms. I cannot clear them for the next mission.
Toxin exposure. I need a lab to identify it before it spreads.
neutral: Vitals are stable. For now.
Holding. No improvement, no decline.
Triage call. I move on, I come back when there's time.
Sleep cycle is broken across the crew. Performance will slip.
Stocks are adequate. We won't run out this rotation.
Standard physiology. The species charts are accurate enough.

positive: Good fight. They won't be coming back.
Clean kills. No survivors to talk.
That position is ours. Hold it.
Drop their leader and the rest fold. Confirmed.
Field belongs to us. Salvage what's useful.
They broke first. They always break first.
warning: We are outnumbered. But that has never stopped us before.
Heavy weapons on overwatch. We move, we die.
They've got the high ground. We need to flank or fall back.
Ammo is low. Make every shot count.
They're not retreating. They want us in close.
That's not local muscle. That's a contract.
neutral: Keep your weapon ready. Always.
Standard patrol pattern. Predictable.
Numbers say nothing. Positioning says everything.
Cover here is thin. We move, we expose.
Quiet. Too quiet means it's coming.
Sentries are sloppy. Could go either way.

positive: Interface looks clean. We can work with this.
Bypassed the lock in under a minute. Old firmware.
Signal is strong. We can route through their array.
Hardware is salvageable. Bring a crate.
Found a backup partition. Their whole operation is documented.
Patch is holding. System is stable end to end.
warning: This system is failing. Cascade is going to take everything if we don't isolate it.
Encryption is military grade. We're not getting in without the key.
Power draw spiked. Something is waking up.
Reactor coolant is venting. Minutes, not hours.
Network is monitored. We trip a sensor, they come running.
Firmware is sabotaged. Whoever was here didn't want us looking.
neutral: Specs are within tolerance. Nothing remarkable.
Standard issue. No surprises in the schematic.
Baseline diagnostic clears. I'll deep-scan if there's time.
Power signature matches civilian. Nothing to flag.
Cooling cycle is fine. Filters need swapping next port.
Logs are clean. Whoever owned this kept it tidy.

positive: Tracks lead the way we want. Whoever was here, they're long gone.
Found a clean route. No sentries, no sensors.
Approach vector is open. We can be on top of them before they hear us.
Sign is old. We're alone out here.
Wind is with us. Sound and scent both.
High ground is ours for the taking.
warning: Something has been watching us for a while. I do not like it.
Tracks loop back. Whoever made them knows we're following.
Predator sign all over this draw. Local fauna or worse.
Smoke on the ridge. Camp, or a signal.
Recent boot prints. Multiple. Heading our way.
That valley is a kill box. Don't take the easy path.
neutral: Terrain is what we expected. No surprises.
Three approaches. Pick the one that suits your crew.
Visibility drops past the tree line. Plan accordingly.
Local wildlife scattered an hour ago. Whatever spooked them is gone too.
Weather will turn before nightfall. Move now or wait it out.
No human sign in any direction. Just dust.

positive: They like us. That is rarer than it sounds.
Reputation traveled ahead of us. Doors open before we knock.
They're talking like we're old friends. We are now.
Patron is pleased. Pleased patrons pay extra.
Won the room. We could ask for the moon and get it.
Story I told sells itself. They want the next chapter.
warning: He's playing a long game. Whatever this is, it's personal — and that makes him predictable.
They're stalling. Either reinforcements, or a trap, or both.
Eye contact says one thing, hands say another. Don't trust this.
Reputation precedes us. The wrong reputation.
Patron just laughed at our price. We have a problem.
Word on the street tags us. Anonymity is gone.
neutral: Talk is cheap. Let's hear what they actually want.
They want to negotiate. So we negotiate.
Polite, careful, noncommittal. Standard fare.
Bar is full of people pretending not to listen.
This is a relationship. Don't burn it for short money.
Quiet town. Means everyone here knows everyone here.
